speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How yet resolves the Governor of the town?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: This is the latest parle we will admit.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Therefore to our best mercy give yourselves

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or, like to men proud of destruction,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Defy us to our worst. For, as I am a soldier,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: A name that in my thoughts becomes me best,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If I begin the batt’ry once again,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I will not leave the half-achieved Harfleur

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Till in her ashes she lie burièd.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The gates of mercy shall be all shut up,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And the fleshed soldier, rough and hard of heart,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In liberty of bloody hand, shall range

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With conscience wide as hell, mowing like grass

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your fresh fair virgins and your flow’ring infants.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is it then to me if impious war,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Arrayed in flames like to the prince of fiends,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do with his smirched complexion all fell feats

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Enlinked to waste and desolation?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What is ’t to me, when you yourselves are cause,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If your pure maidens fall into the hand

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of hot and forcing violation?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What rein can hold licentious wickedness

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: When down the hill he holds his fierce career?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: We may as bootless spend our vain command

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Upon th’ enragèd soldiers in their spoil

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: As send precepts to the Leviathan

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To come ashore. Therefore, you men of Harfleur,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Take pity of your town and of your people

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whiles yet my soldiers are in my command,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whiles yet the cool and temperate wind of grace

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O’erblows the filthy and contagious clouds

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Of heady murder, spoil, and villainy.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If not, why, in a moment look to see

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The blind and bloody soldier with foul hand

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Desire the locks of your shrill-shrieking daughters,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your fathers taken by the silver beards

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And their most reverend heads dashed to the walls,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Your naked infants spitted upon pikes

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whiles the mad mothers with their howls confused

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Do break the clouds, as did the wives of Jewry

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: At Herod’s bloody-hunting slaughtermen.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What say you? Will you yield and this avoid

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Or, guilty in defense, be thus destroyed?

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: Our expectation hath this day an end.

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: The Dauphin, whom of succors we entreated,

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: Returns us that his powers are yet not ready

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: To raise so great a siege. Therefore, great king,

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: We yield our town and lives to thy soft mercy.

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: Enter our gates, dispose of us and ours,

speaker: GOVERNOR
text: For we no longer are defensible.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Open your gates.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, uncle Exeter,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go you and enter Harfleur. There remain,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And fortify it strongly ’gainst the French.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Use mercy to them all for us, dear uncle.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The winter coming on and sickness growing

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Upon our soldiers, we will retire to Calais.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tonight in Harfleur will we be your guest.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Tomorrow for the march are we addressed.
